title: Amazon 2026 Proxy Statement — first 20 pages (FrameGraph replica)
description: Structural replica of pp. 1–20 of the Amazon.com, Inc. 2026 Notice & Proxy Statement. Director photos and the amazon wordmark/arrow are trademarks, shown as labelled placeholders. Dense overview-page bullet lists are representative samples; bios pp. 14–20 use one data-driven template.

objects.text:
Notice of 2026
Annual Meeting
of Shareholders
& Proxy Statement
(cover — brand image and amazon wordmark shown as placeholders; the arrow mark is a trademark)
To Be Held on Wednesday, May 20, 2026

Thank you for being an Amazon shareholder. No matter how large or small your holdings may be, your vote is important to us, and we encourage you to vote your shares in accordance with the Board’s recommendations. The information here is only an overview—you can learn more by reading our Proxy Statement and Annual Report.
2. Ratification of Ernst & Young as independent auditors
3. Advisory vote to approve executive compensation
of stock owned by holders the lead independent director met with

items: We have a single class of common stock with equal voting rights (one share, one vote).
We have a declassified board—all directors are elected annually.
We have a majority voting standard for the election of directors.
We have a lead independent director appointed by the independent directors.
We prohibit hedging, speculative, and derivative transactions by directors and officers.
Shareholders owning at least 25% of shares may call a special meeting.
Our philosophy is anchored on periodic grants of time-vested RSUs that vest over the long term, aligning executive compensation with the returns we deliver to shareholders.
At our 2025 Annual Meeting, 78% of votes cast supported our say-on-pay advisory vote.
The Committee granted no equity to our CEO or named executives in 2025, and none to our CEO since 2021.

objects.text: [Representative selection of the source’s governance and compensation highlight bullets.]

items: We support a wide range of programs that help thousands of charities and communities worldwide.
Our Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee oversees corporate social responsibility risks.

objects.text: 5  Additional Reporting on Impact of Data Centers on Climate Commitments

items: Our current public reporting already addresses the proposal’s concerns, making the report unnecessary.
In 2024, carbon intensity fell for the sixth consecutive year, down 4% on 11% business growth.

objects.text: 6  Report on Impact of Climate Commitments

items: We evaluate and adapt our sustainability approach to navigate evolving demands.
The additional financial reporting requested would be impractical and potentially misleading.
We are committed to strong, independent leadership of the Board; our lead independent director reinforces independent oversight.
Our governance guidelines let the Board determine the optimal leadership structure for Amazon at any given time.
Our current structure provides robust risk oversight by independent directors.

objects.text: We obsess over making customers’ lives better and easier with sharp pricing, vast selection, and record delivery speeds.

items: Delivered over 13 billion items the same or next day globally to Prime members—our fastest speeds ever.
Named the lowest-priced U.S. retailer by Profitero, on average 14% lower than other major retailers.
Scaled the Rufus AI shopping assistant to more than 300 million customers.
Trainium2 fully subscribed with 1.4 million chips landed; launched Trainium3, up to 40% more price-performant.
Expanded the Amazon Leo low-Earth-orbit fleet to more than 200 satellites.

objects.text: With more than one million U.S. employees, we have created more jobs in the country than any other company over the past decade.

items: Investing over $1 billion in U.S. pay and benefits—average total comp above $30/hour including elected benefits.
Global Recordable Incident Rate improved 43% over six years and 14% year over year.
Invested more than $2.5 billion to advance safety progress since 2019.
Announced Future Ready 2030: a $2.5 billion goal to train 50 million people globally.

objects.text: The Climate Pledge is our goal to reach net-zero carbon emissions by 2040, with more than 640 signatories across 60+ industries.

items: Reached a global data-center PUE of 1.15 in 2024 (best site 1.04).
Custom In-Row Heat Exchanger is 20% more power efficient and uses 9% less water than air-cooled sites.
Invested in more than 700 carbon-free energy projects worldwide (40+ GW capacity).
Removed all plastic air pillows from delivery packaging globally.
Deployed more than 40,000 electric delivery vehicles globally.

objects.text: Our U.S. investments reached more than $340 billion in 2025, contributing more than $1.8 trillion to the U.S. economy since 2010.

items: 134,000+ employees across 55 countries volunteered during our Global Month of Volunteering.
Delivered more than 60 million free meals across the U.S. and UK.
Committed $3.6 billion to keep 35,000 homes affordable for 99 years.
Since 2017, delivered 26 million+ relief items across 200+ disasters.
Responded to 30 natural disasters across 12 countries in 2025.

objects.text:
NOTICE OF 2026 ANNUAL MEETING
OF SHAREHOLDERS
Our Board of Directors Recommends You Vote:
To elect the eleven directors named in the Proxy Statement to serve until the next Annual Meeting

spans: the election of each director nominee

objects.text: To ratify the appointment of Ernst & Young LLP as our independent auditors for fiscal year 2026
To conduct an advisory vote to approve our executive compensation
To consider and act upon the shareholder proposals described in the Proxy Statement
To transact such other business as may properly come before the meeting
The Board of Directors has fixed March 26, 2026 as the record date for determining shareholders entitled to notice of, and to vote at, the Annual Meeting.
By Order of the Board of Directors

spans.text: Important Notice Regarding the Availability of Proxy Materials.

spans: The Proxy Statement and our 2025 Annual Report are available at

objects.text: ITEM 3—Advisory Vote to Approve Executive Compensation

This document includes forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. All statements other than statements of historical or current facts are forward-looking. Forward-looking statements reflect management’s current expectations and are inherently uncertain; actual results could differ materially. See our 2025 Annual Report on Form 10-K for risks and uncertainties.
Annual Meeting of Shareholders — To Be Held on Wednesday, May 20, 2026

The enclosed proxy is solicited by the Board of Directors of Amazon.com, Inc. (“Amazon” or the “Company”) for the Annual Meeting of Shareholders to be held virtually at 9:00 a.m., Pacific Time, on Wednesday, May 20, 2026, and any adjournment or postponement thereof.
Voting via the Internet, mobile device, or by telephone helps save money by reducing postage and proxy tabulation costs. Each method is available 24 hours per day, seven days per week.
We encourage you to cast your vote by one of the following methods:
See Notice or Voting Instruction Form
See Notice or Voting Instruction Form
Our principal offices are located at 410 Terry Avenue North, Seattle, Washington 98109. This Proxy Statement is first being made available to our shareholders on or about April 9, 2026.

In accordance with our Bylaws, the Board has fixed the number of directors at eleven as of the Annual Meeting. Based on the recommendation of the Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee, the Board proposes that the following eleven nominees be elected, each to hold office until the next Annual Meeting or until a successor is elected and qualified:
Each nominee is currently a director and was most recently elected at the 2025 Annual Meeting. On April 7, 2026, Keith B. Alexander informed the Company that he would not stand for re-election.
Why We Recommend You Support This Proposal

items: We have the appropriate mix of skills, qualifications, backgrounds, and tenures on the Board.
Our composition balances longer-serving directors with fresh perspectives from newer directors.
The Board actively oversees sustainability, governance, risk, and regulatory compliance.

objects.text: The Board of Directors recommends a vote “FOR” each nominee.

In evaluating the nominees, the Board and the Nominating and Corporate Governance Committee consider the qualities they seek for directors and each director’s individual qualifications, skills, expertise, and background in the context of the Company’s operations, so that collectively the Board operates as a diverse, engaged, and effective body.
Founder and Executive Chair of Amazon

Mr. Bezos’s individual qualifications and skills as a director include his customer-focused point of view, his willingness to encourage invention, his long-term perspective, and his ongoing contributions as founder and Executive Chair.

Mr. Bezos has been Chair of the Board since founding the Company in 1994. Prior to becoming Executive Chair in July 2021, he served as Chief Executive Officer from May 1996 to July 2021 and as President from founding until June 1999 and again from October 2000 to July 2021.

items: Executive Chair of the Bezos Earth Fund, founded with a $10 billion commitment to fight climate change.
Founded the Bezos Day One Fund, a $2 billion commitment for homelessness and preschools.
Founded Blue Origin; owns The Washington Post; co-founded AI company Project Prometheus (co-CEO since November 2025).

objects.text: (director photo is a placeholder; the source page reproduces a portrait)

Mr. Jassy’s customer-focused and long-term-oriented approach, and his deep knowledge across all of the Company’s businesses—including as the first CEO of Amazon Web Services—provide the Board a unified vision of operations and long-term strategy and key insights into the Company’s strategy, challenges, and successes.

Mr. Jassy has been President and CEO since July 2021. He founded and led AWS since its inception in 2006, serving as its CEO from April 2016 to July 2021. He joined the Company in 1997 and held various leadership roles before founding AWS.

items: Chair and founding member of the board of Rainier Prep, a charter middle school.
Member of the National Academy of Engineering.

objects.text: (director photo is a placeholder; the source page reproduces a portrait)
Co-Founder of Medley Living, Inc. and Former EVP of Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.

Ms. Cooper brings deep human capital management expertise, including nine years as EVP, Global Head of Human Capital Management of Goldman Sachs, and the perspective of overseeing rapidly growing companies as well as large international companies across technology, consumer, and financial fields.
Ms. Cooper is a co-founder of Medley Living (launched September 2020) and served as EVP, Global Head of Human Capital Management of Goldman Sachs from March 2008 to December 2017.

items: Director of PepsiCo, Inc. since September 2021; former director of Etsy and Slack Technologies.
Trustee of the Museum of Modern Art since 2017.

objects.text: (director photo is a placeholder; the source page reproduces a portrait)
Lead Independent Director of Amazon; Senior Counsel with WilmerHale

Ms. Gorelick brings a unique perspective on domestic and international public policy and government regulation through her experience in the federal government and at a leading law firm, helping the Board anticipate and navigate governance and policy matters.

Ms. Gorelick has been Senior Counsel at WilmerHale since January 2026 (Partner 2003–2025). She served as Deputy Attorney General, General Counsel of the Department of Defense, and on the bipartisan 9/11 Commission.

items: Director of VeriSign, Inc. since January 2015.
Chair of the Urban Institute (2014–2024); director (2004–2024).

objects.text: (director photo is a placeholder; the source page reproduces a portrait)
Dean of MIT Schwarzman College of Computing

Dr. Huttenlocher’s experience as an internationally-recognized computer scientist at MIT, Cornell, Cornell Tech, and Xerox PARC puts him at the forefront of emerging computing technologies and gives the Board deep insight into artificial intelligence as a transforming technology.

Dr. Huttenlocher has been Dean of MIT Schwarzman College of Computing since August 2019. He was Dean and Vice Provost of Cornell Tech from 2012 to July 2019 and held various Cornell positions from 1988.

items: Director of Corning Incorporated since February 2015.
Board member of the MacArthur Foundation (2010–2022), chair 2018–2022.

objects.text: (director photo is a placeholder; the source page reproduces a portrait)
Managing General Partner, AI Fund LP; Founder, DeepLearning.AI

Dr. Ng is a global leader in both AI and education. His academic and private-sector work developing machine learning and deep learning algorithms informs the Board’s perspective on the opportunities and challenges that AI presents and its transformative social and business potential.

Dr. Ng has been Managing General Partner of AI Fund since January 2018 and has led DeepLearning.AI since June 2017. He is Executive Chairman of LandingAI and co-founded Coursera, where he has served as Chairman since 2014.

items: Chief Scientist & VP of Baidu, Inc. (2014–2017).

objects.text: (director photo is a placeholder; the source page reproduces a portrait)
Former Chair and CEO of PepsiCo, Inc.

Mrs. Nooyi’s leadership over a 25-year career at PepsiCo—including as CEO, President, and CFO—helps the Board oversee a large corporation with international operations, including financial planning, capital allocation, marketing, and accounting, and assess consumer-focused product development.
Mrs. Nooyi was CEO of PepsiCo from October 2006 to October 2018 and Chair from May 2007 to February 2019. She became President and CFO in 2001 after joining PepsiCo in 1994.

items: Director of Honeywell International Inc. since January 2026; director of Royal Philips since May 2021.
Trustee of Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center since 2020.
